scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: The research topic on traceable autonomous research agents is feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM, as the MPS GPU can handle large language models and the ample RAM allows for processing substantial datasets. Suitable public datasets include the ArXiv Open Access Subset (for scientific papers), PubMed Central (for biomedical literature), and the Wikipedia Dumps (for general knowledge). Experimentation would likely take 2-4 hours for initial model training and 10-30 minutes per test run, de

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
parsed_papers.core_claim: The AI Scientist generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process in an automated, iterative fashion.
method_or_focus: The development of a fully automated system that can perform the entire scientific discovery process from idea generation to paper writing and evaluation.
parsed_papers.best_result: Papers exceeding acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: The development of a fully automated system that can perform the entire scientific discovery process from idea generation to paper writing and evaluation.

deep_read.best_result: Papers exceeding acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
parsed_papers.core_claim: Extends automated scientific discovery with stronger search and review-like iteration, motivating stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search

key_components: Eliminates reliance on human-authored code templates
Generalizes across diverse machine learning domains
Progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by experiment manager agent
Enhanced AI reviewer with Vision-Language Model (VLM) feedback loop for figure refinement

evaluation.achievement: One manuscript exceeded average human acceptance threshold, first fully AI-generated paper to successfully navigate peer review

parsed_papers.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
parsed_papers.core_claim: Describes agentic research assistance across literature review, experimentation, and report writing, highlighting the need for human oversight and reproducibility controls.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
deep_read.method: Agent Laboratory is a framework that accepts human-provided research ideas and progresses through three stages: literature review, experimentation, and report writing to produce comprehensive research outputs, including a code repository and a research report. Users can provide feedback and guidance at each stage.
deep_read.implementation: Agent Laboratory was deployed with various state-of-the-art LLMs. Multiple researchers participated in a survey to assess quality, provided human feedback to guide the research process, and evaluated the final papers.
findings: Agent Laboratory driven by o1-preview generates the best research outcomes
The generated machine learning code achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing methods
Human involvement, providing feedback at each stage, significantly improves the overall quality of research
Agent Laboratory significantly reduces research expenses, achieving an 84% decrease compared to previous autonomous research methods

parsed_papers.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
parsed_papers.core_claim: Created a benchmark using Kaggle competitions to evaluate ML agents on real-world ML engineering tasks, using human baselines from Kaggle leaderboards and open-source agent scaffolds.
method_or_focus: MLE-bench, a benchmark that evaluates ML agents on real-world Kaggle competitions using human baselines.
parsed_papers.best_result: OpenAI's o1-preview with AIDE scaffolding achieved at least the level of a Kaggle bronze medal in 16.9% of competitions.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: MLE-bench, a benchmark that evaluates ML agents on real-world Kaggle competitions using human baselines.

parsed_papers.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
parsed_papers.core_claim: AIBuildAI-2 is a knowledge-enhanced agent with an external, evolving knowledge system for automatically building AI models, featuring a hierarchical organization of curated AI development knowledge with dynamic context loading.
method_or_focus: A hierarchical, evolving knowledge system that allows the agent to dynamically load relevant context and ground decisions in externally verifiable expertise while continuously learning from its own experiences.

parsed_papers.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
parsed_papers.core_claim: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

method.name: Reflexion
method.description: An approach that uses verbal reinforcement to help agents learn from prior failings by converting binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback, which is added as additional context for the LLM agent in the next episode.
feedback_generation_methods: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation using LLMs (decision-making) or self-written unit tests (programming)

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows more nuanced forms of feedback compared to scalar rewards
More explicit and interpretable episodic memory over prior experiences
Provides more explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities

experiments.description: Sequential action choices over long trajectories
Teaching agents to effectively use external tools
experiments.improvement: Not specified in the provided text

parsed_papers.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
parsed_papers.core_claim: Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.method_description: An iterative self-refinement algorithm that alternates between feedback and refine steps, using the same language model for both generating feedback and refining outputs without additional training or external supervision.
key_characteristics: Uses the same language model for both generating feedback and refining outputs
Does not require external supervision or reward models
Uses few-shot prompting to guide the model
Iterates until a specified number of iterations or until the model determines no further refinement is needed
algorithm_steps: Generate an initial output from model M
Pass the output back to the same model to get feedback
Pass the feedback back to the same model to refine the previously generated output
Repeat steps 2-3 until stopping condition is met

evaluation.results: Outperforms direct generation from strong LLMs

deep_read.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback

parsed_papers.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
parsed_papers.core_claim: Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
deep_read.abstract: Introduces Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent. It operates in Minecraft to autonomously drive exploration, master a wide range of skills, and make new discoveries without human intervention.
deep_read.methodology: Voyager is an LLM-powered agent that uses an automatic curriculum, a skill library, and an iterative prompting mechanism. It interacts with a blackbox LLM (GPT-4) via prompting and in-context learning, using code as the action space and avoiding the need for model parameter access or gradient-based training.
key_components: An automatic curriculum for open-ended exploration
A skill library for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
An iterative prompting mechanism that generates executable code for embodied control
key_innovations: First LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent
Continuously explores, plans, and develops new skills in open-ended environments
Makes new discoveries without human intervention
Uses code as the action space for complex, long-horizon tasks
deep_read.results: Voyager discovers new Minecraft items and skills continually through self-driven exploration and significantly outperforms baseline methods. The agent demonstrates capabilities such as proposing suitable tasks based on its skill level, refining skills based on feedback, and continually exploring the world in a self-driven manner.

parsed_papers.core_claim: Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

deep_read.paper_title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering

method.approach: Investigation of how interface design affects LM agent performance
method.system: SWE-agent, a system that facilitates LM agents to autonomously use computers for software engineering tasks
key_features: Enhanced ability to create and edit code files

evaluation.performance: State-of-the-art, exceeding previous results with non-interactive LMs

method.insights: How ACI design impacts agent behavior and performance

research_gaps.text: Traceability gap: agents may produce ideas without an auditable chain from source evidence to experiment decision.
Review gap: simulated reviewer feedback is often generated after the fact instead of controlling whether an idea proceeds.
Experiment timing gap: many research agents run experiments late, after committing to an idea, rather than using cheap probes during ideation.
Failure memory gap: rejected ideas and failed probes are often lost instead of shaping the next search iteration.
Venue-fit gap: agent outputs often lack explicit comparison to workshop and conference expectations.
